verbs.summary: Ἰακὼβ ἐγέννησεν (begot) τὸν Ἰωσὴφ, describing the act of Jacob giving birth to Joseph as part of the family line.
Ἰησοῦς ἐγεννήθη (was begotten) ἐξ ἧς, indicating that Jesus was born from Mary (as the source named in the sentence).
Ἰησοῦς ὁ λεγόμενος Χριστός states that Jesus is referred to as “Christ,” marking the ongoing/attributed naming implied by “the one called Christ.”
